channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Firefighters race to contain Los Angeles wildfires with menacing winds forecast to return
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters raced Saturday to cut off spreading wildfires before potentially strong winds return that could push the flames toward the world famous J. Paul Getty Museum and the University of California, Los Angeles, while new evacuation warnings left more homeowners on edge.
item.dc:creator: JAE HONG, HOLLY RAMER and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, WGN 9

item.title: Mayotte on red alert again as another cyclone heads toward the French territory off Africa
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The French territory of Mayotte was put on red alert Saturday as another cyclone headed toward the islands off Africa that were devastated by their worst storm in nearly a century last month. The Prefecture of Mayotte said the red alert warning was issued at 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Special counsel Jack Smith has resigned after submitting his Trump report, Justice Department says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Special counsel Jack Smith has resigned from the Justice Department after submitting his investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump, an expected move that comes amid legal wrangling over how much of that document can be made public in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Powerful winter storm that dumped snow in US South maintains its icy grip
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Flight cancellations piled up and officials warned of continuing dangerous roads Saturday in the wake of a winter storm that brought biting cold and wet snow to the U.S. South, leading to school closures and disrupting travel.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, KREX 5

item.title: Pence said he congratulated Trump on his win at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said he congratulated President-elect Trump on his upcoming return to the White House when the two shared a brief greeting at former President Jimmy Carter’s funeral service on Thursday. “He greeted me when he came down the aisle. I stood up, extended my hand.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LA fire chief says city failed department amid ongoing Southern California wildfires
item.description: Los Angeles Fire Department (LAFD) Chief Kristin Crowley said during an interview Friday that the city failed the department amid the ongoing wildfires in Southern California that have taken at least 11 lives and burned more than 37,000 acres.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: NBA postpones Rockets vs. Hawks game because of winter storm
item.description: The game between the Houston Rockets and Atlanta Hawks scheduled for Saturday has been postponed because of a winter storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Are there no Michelin-starred restaurants near you? There's a reason for that
item.description: You'd have difficulty finding Michelin-starred restaurants in most states — but it's not because there aren't excellent eaters in those places.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Many Democrats don't think they'll see a woman become president, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: An increasing number of Democrats are questioning whether their party’s commitment to diverse candidates, especially women, may lead to more political struggles in the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Power grid faults surged right before Los Angeles wildfires began: expert
item.description: A company that monitors electrical activity says faults along the Los Angeles power grid soared in the same areas where three of this week’s major wildfires are occurring.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: City's infamous red tape preventing private security from helping homeowners, firms say
item.description: Los Angeles law enforcement has warned that looters will be arrested and prosecuted aggressively as wildfires scorch Los Angeles and prompted 180K evacuations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died
item.description: Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died. He was 84.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints displaced by LA wildfires
item.description: Thousands of Latter-day Saints have been evacuated and displaced due to the wildfires around the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, ABC 4

item.title: Californians scrambled to evacuate dogs, cats, horses, even pigs as LA flames closed in
item.description: Animal owners in Los Angeles have scrambled to get themselves and their beloved companions out of the way of fast-moving wildfires this week that have incinerated whole neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden And Leslie Ambriz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Powerful winter storm dumps heavy snow, causing flight delays, slick roads and school closings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into Southern U.S. states Friday, making for dicey travel and a rare snow day for many students.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass rejects assistance from FDNY; Newsom accepts help from Mexico crews
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass recently rejected an offer for assistance from the New York Fire Department, outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom calls for investigation into water supply after tanks 'went dry' during wildfire
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has ordered an investigation into the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power amid reports of a loss of water pressure to fire hydrants and limited water resources in the wildfire zones.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, WGN 9

item.title: Navy veteran cleared of conspiracy charges in high-profile Jan. 6 case is sentenced to time served
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired Navy intelligence officer who was cleared of the most serious charges in his U.S. Capitol attack trial alongside Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes avoided additional time behind bars when he was sentenced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Disney pledges $15 million in Los Angeles fire aid as more stars learn they've lost their homes
item.description: Disney says it will donate $15 million to respond to the fires and help rebuild. The company announced the donations Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How a week of weather extremes upended the lives of millions of Americans
item.description: The week began with a biting cold snap that hammered much of the U.S., the product of a jet stream that slips out of its usual path more often these days.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Winter storm lets Southern states enjoy a rare snow day
item.description: When life gives you a snow day, make a snowman. Across the U.S. South, millions of residents woke up Friday to a rare chance to bust out the mittens and snow pants thanks to a cold snap that dumped snow in their parks, streets and backyards.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, TRAVIS LOLLER and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida woman messages investigator instead of drug dealer by mistake
item.description: A woman landed herself in hot water when she texted someone she believed was a drug dealer, but accidentally messaged a Bay County narcotics investigator instead.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, Fox 5

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt for defaming Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge on Friday held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt for skirting an agreement not to make new false claims about two Georgia election workers he was ordered to pay $146 million for defaming.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Praying in burning street 'was instinct': California fire survivor posts viral video in fiery embers
item.description: A Minnesota storm chaser recently went viral on social media for a video he posted praying over a fiery street during the Palisades Fire in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Excerpts from President-elect Donald Trump’s sentencing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump spoke for six minutes. The judge talked for seven. In the end, the first criminal court sentencing of a former or future president took little more than half an hour.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Civilian drone hits, damages firefighting aircraft over Palisades Fire
item.description: A firefighting aircraft that was operating over the Palisades Fire is grounded and out of service after it was struck and damaged by a civilian’s drone on Thursday afternoon, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Illegal immigrant accused in attempted teen kidnapping deported 5 times: officials
item.description: A Mexican illegal immigrant deported from the United States five times has been arrested after allegedly attempting to kidnap a teenage Alabama girl on New Year's Eve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 notable moments between VIPs at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former President Carter’s funeral service at Washington National Cathedral brought together all five living presidents and current and former officials for an event full of notable interactions.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Anita Bryant, anti-gay rights activist and singer, dead at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant Dry, known popularly as Anita Bryant — a singer and crusader against the progress of gay rights — has died at the age of 84, her family announced in an obituary.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Far from wildfire flames, residents of sunny Los Angeles go about their lives in disquiet and fear
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Pedestrians shuffled by the famed Chateau Marmont hotel, customers queued up at Starbucks on Sunset Boulevard and car horns bleated at gridlocked intersections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4
